intro.text: Elara woke slowly, the scent of velvet and night-blooming roses filling her senses. She was not in a cold prison cell.
Instead, she lay in an elegant four-poster bed draped in deep pink silk.
This was clearly someone’s personal chamber of a luxurious, and unmistakably a vampire’s.
Her hand instinctively went to her neck. The bite mark was still warm… and strangely sensitive.
A faint scent of roses lingers in the air.
Where… am I? This isn’t a dungeon… This is her room.

intro.speaker: Elara
intro.text: (whispering to herself, voice shaky) Where… am I? This isn’t a dungeon… This is her room.
Heart pounding, she sat up. Escape was her only thought. She scanned the dimly lit chamber.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: Hmm? There is a diary book? I am curious what's inside... Maybe I'll take a peek.
Elara carefully opened the diary.
The handwriting was elegant and flowing, with occasional small doodles of flowers and desserts in the margins. As she read, her eyes widened little by little.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: (whispering to herself) So… her name is Lilith Voss. Born in the Crimson Palace… She doesn’t remember her real mother...

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: Queen Charlotte adopted her and raised her as a commander of the lesser vampires...
She flipped through more pages, heart beating faster.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: She likes strawberry cakes and warm pumpkin pie…? That’s… surprisingly normal for a vampire like her.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: I thought she only drank blood and ate human flesh or something.
Elara suddenly froze as she reached a recent entry.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: (eyes widening) Wait… these cakes? The pumpkin pie with cinnamon? I made those! This is the same order my mother’s bakery received every full moon…”
A quiet, almost fond smile tugged at her lips despite everything.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: (softly) So that mysterious customer in the black lace dress who always came at night… was her? She’s been eating my baking this whole time…

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: She hesitated for a moment, then picked up the quill that lay beside the diary.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: (murmuring while writing) I am glad you enjoyed the bread and cakes I made for everyone…
She added a small doodle of a strawberry next to her words, then gently closed the book.
A strange warmth bloomed in her chest. Even after everything that happened to Meadowbrook…
knowing Lilith had secretly enjoyed her baking made the vampire feel a little more human.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: These are… so beautiful. I’ve never seen dresses like this before. They look like something from a dark fairy tale…
She reached out and gently touched the fabric. It felt incredibly soft despite its dramatic appearance.
For a brief moment, she forgot the danger she was in.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: (talking to herself, flustered) I wonder if this would even fit me… Wait—no! What am I thinking? There’s no time for this. I need to find a way out!
Still, her fingers lingered on one particularly elegant black-and-crimson dress with delicate lace trims and soft velvet accents.
She turned toward the tall mirror and stared at her reflection, cheeks turning pink.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: (softly, amazed) This is… very beautiful. I look so different… almost like I belong here.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: The door is unlocked. The one who captured me didn't bother to keep me here... I wonder why?

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: Better to explore the room first.
Elara's hand trembled as she grasped the cold brass handle.
With a deep breath, she pulled the door open...
Beyond the threshold lay shadows she couldn't yet understand.
Her journey was only beginning.
